content.divergence_summary: The fork is a single missed meal — which sounds small until you see what it costs. In the main branch, YY woke into a morning already behind, announced the injustice to no one, and carried the hunger forward unresolved. In the alt branch, YY woke on time, ate, read about quantum time on World Quantum Day, and found the day's unremarkableness faintly interesting. Same world event, same character, same April morning. One version has a burden that didn't need to be there.
content.branch_a_path: YY overslept, missed breakfast, felt the wrongness acutely, said so out loud, and moved on carrying the weight.
content.branch_b_path: YY woke on time, ate, encountered the same time-themed day without disruption, and filed it away as interesting.
key_differences: Hunger: main carries missed_breakfast as an active burden; alt resolved it before the day started
Attention: main ends at 0.50 (dampened by low-grade dissatisfaction); alt ends at 0.75 (no drag)
Emotional register: main is expressive and slightly aggrieved; alt is observant and neutral
shared_elements: Same world anchor — World Quantum Day, time as the underlying theme
Same core restlessness and curiosity; neither branch produces a day of consequence
Both versions of YY notice the morning rather than just passing through it
